channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
item.description:
Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards.
The flaws m...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
item.description: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and Andro...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing traditio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NetNut proxy network disrupted, 2 million infected devices cut off
item.description: A joint operation involving Google has disrupted NetNut, a residential proxy network that gave access to millions of compromised Android devices, including smart TVs and streaming boxes. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description:
Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft.
According to JF...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform dubbed "ARToken" appears to operate as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing platform, giving researchers a glimpse into an extensive toolkit designed to...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
item.description:
A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan.
"...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Chinese LLMs Broaden the Gap Between Attackers & Defenders
item.description: Two new models from Chinese firms compete with top US mainstream and frontier models. Should cyber-defenders be worried?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked With Pegasus
item.description: A new report from the Citizen Lab has revealed that former Member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou had his mobile device repeatedly hacked with the notorious Pegasus spyware while serving ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data.
The stealer, discovered by...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Claude Fable 5 isn’t permanently leaving subscriptions, Anthropic says
item.description: Anthropic says Claude Fable 5 won't be accessible via Claude subscriptions after July 7, but it's not a permanent change, and the company expects the model to return outside the usage-based plan soon....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Fable relaunch disappoints users with nerfed performance
item.description: Claude Fable, the company's most powerful model, is now available to all users, but early impressions are disappointing, as it appears to be nowhere near the original release. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
item.description: Improved institutional safeguards and stricter regulations have pushed the burdens of protection and risk reduction on to Australian businesses....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Apple Reverses Age-Old Patch Policy to Keep Up With AI
item.description: Expect more compressed patching cycles from Apple going forward, as attackers leverage artificial intelligence to reduce time to exploit....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicl...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
item.description: After gaining a foothold in thousands of Fortinet firewalls, the attackers are starting to monetize that access, and are also piling on a Nextcloud zero-day bug....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
item.description:
Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic.
Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat I...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description:
Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access.
"Although tactics differ between...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ransomware Thugs Masquerade as Interpol to Entice Small Biz
item.description: The ransomware campaign relies on basic social engineering and stretches across multiple regions, including the US, Europe, Middle East, and elsewhere....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description:
This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots.
Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google loses final appeal to overturn €4.1 billion EU fine
item.description: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Google's final appeal against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine over the company's use of Android to promote its Chrome browser a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
item.description: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks steal Microsoft 365 tokens in seconds using fake prompts and OAuth flows. Learn how these MFA bypass tactics work and how to defend against them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
item.description:
The threat actor known as ToddyCat has been attributed to a new malware called Umbrij that's designed to gain surreptitious access to a victim's email correspondence via the Google API.
"In this camp...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic's AI Finds Bugs. IBM Bets $5B It Can Fix Them.
item.description: IBM and Red Hat assign 20,000 engineers to the new Project Lightwell service as Anthropic's Mythos findings ignite debate over how to secure the open-source software supply chain....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft fixes bug that removed Copilot buttons in Outlook
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing the Copilot Chat or Copilot buttons in Classic Outlook to disappear for Windows users with the Copilot Chat (Basic) license. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco finally confirms attackers exploiting Unified CM flaw
item.description: Cisco confirmed that attackers are now exploiting a Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) vulnerability patched in early June. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
item.description: Identity lifecycle management was architected around a person with an employment record, a manager, and a departure date. AI agents have none of those. As autonomous principals proliferate across ente...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA: Microsoft SharePoint RCE flaw now actively exploited
item.description: CISA warned on Wednesday that attackers have begun exploiting a high-severity Microsoft SharePoint remote code execution vulnerability patched in May. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Opera rolls out Paste Protect feature to fight ClickFix attacks
item.description: Opera has introduced Paste Protect, a security feature designed to block ClickFix-style attacks that trick users into executing malicious commands through social engineering. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Security firm Sysdig says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent.
Its Threat Research Team calls the operator JADEPUFFER and says a large...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
